title: Chemistry Foundations
description: Learn and master the concepts of Chemistry Foundations with structured lessons, interactive visualizations, and practice exercises.

title: Atomic Structure
description: Learn and master the concepts of Atomic Structure with structured lessons, interactive visualizations, and practice exercises.

title: Periodic Table
description: Learn and master the concepts of Periodic Table with structured lessons, interactive visualizations, and practice exercises.

title: Chemical Bonding
description: Learn and master the concepts of Chemical Bonding with structured lessons, interactive visualizations, and practice exercises.

title: States of Matter
description: Learn and master the concepts of States of Matter with structured lessons, interactive visualizations, and practice exercises.

title: Thermochemistry
description: It forms the bridge between chemistry and thermodynamics and is one of the core physical chemistry courses.

title: Chemical Equilibrium
description: This is another major pillar of physical chemistry.

title: Acids & Bases
description: This is one of the most important chemistry courses because it connects equilibrium with real-world chemical systems.

title: Electrochemistry
description: This is one of the most practical chemistry courses, connecting chemistry with electricity, energy storage, and modern battery technology.

title: Chemical Kinetics
description: This is the final major **Physical Chemistry** course before moving into **Inorganic Chemistry**.

title: Main Group Chemistry
description: From this point onward, the curriculum shifts from physical principles to the chemistry of the elements themselves.

title: Transition Metals
description: This naturally leads into **Coordination Chemistry**, where transition metal complexes are explored in depth.

title: Coordination Chemistry
description: It is one of the most important advanced inorganic chemistry courses.

title: Organic Chemistry I
description: This marks the beginning of the **Organic Chemistry** sequence, one of the largest and most important areas of chemistry.

title: Organic Chemistry II
description: This is where students learn the chemistry of the major functional groups in depth and begin designing organic syntheses.

title: Biomolecules
description: Learn and master the concepts of Biomolecules with structured lessons, interactive visualizations, and practice exercises.

title: Polymers
description: This course explains the chemistry behind plastics, rubbers, fibers, composites, and modern materials.

title: Analytical Chemistry
description: This course teaches **how chemists determine what substances are present and how much is present**, making it essential for research, medicine, environmental science, and industry.

title: Spectroscopy
description: This is one of the most important courses for chemistry research, pharmaceuticals, and molecular identification.

title: Environmental Chemistry
description: This course connects chemistry with environmental science and sustainability.

title: Materials Chemistry
description: These final five courses focus on modern materials, nanotechnology, nuclear science, large-scale manufacturing, and computer-assisted chemistry, completing a comprehensive university-level chemistry curriculum.

title: Nanochemistry
description: This course focuses on chemistry at the **1–100 nanometer scale**, where materials exhibit unique physical and chemical properties.

title: Nuclear Chemistry
description: This course connects chemistry with nuclear physics and energy science.

title: Industrial Chemistry
description: This course focuses on how chemistry is applied at large industrial scales to manufacture products used every day.

title: Computational Chemistry
description: This is the final course and completes a comprehensive university-level chemistry curriculum.
